Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California Pursuant To Ccp 128.7; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-19-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ROBERT CARROLL III, Esq. of Phillips, Erlewine, Given & Carlin LLP (Tel: 415-398-0900) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ALEXANDRA C. SEIBERT, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (Tel: 415-288-9800) for defendant the Regents of the University of California.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PAUL HARVEY's Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California Pursuant To CCP 128.7.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion for sanctions against defendant the Regents of the University of California pursuant to CCP 128.7" is denied.

Plaintiff argues that defendant's summary judgment motion in this medical malpractice case was "frivolous," particularly after plaintiff filed an expert declaration on the standard of care to dispute defendant's expert declaration. I actually found defendant's declaration more forceful than plaintiff's, but denied defendant summary judgment due to the triable facts and issues raised. Defendant's motion was proper and commonplace; there was nothing frivolous or sanctionable about it. No one is awarded any sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR No. 2737, Email: candace.yount@gmail.com, Tel: 415-994-5619 (302/RBU)
